matter.title: Roe v. Coastal Outfitters LLC et al. (Paper Street / Road Access)
matter.summary: Grace C. Roe, a lot owner and officer of the Lakeview Heights Road Association, sues to confirm that Maple Street in the Lakeview Heights plat is a deemed-vacated paper street, to defeat Coastal Outfitters LLC's claim of access over it to a landlocked back lot the developer wants to subdivide, and to collect Maria Q. Fairbanks's unpaid share of the private road maintenance.

plaintiff.role: Plaintiff lot owner (and road association officer)

defendant.role: Defendant neighbor (refuses to pay road maintenance)

defendant_2.role: Defendant developer (claims the paper street for back-lot access)

other_party.role: The municipality (interested in the way's status)

client.role: Plaintiff lot owner (and road association officer)

fact_pattern.summary: Grace C. Roe, a lot owner and officer of the Lakeview Heights Road Association, sues to confirm that Maple Street in the Lakeview Heights plat is a deemed-vacated paper street, to defeat Coastal Outfitters LLC's claim of access over it to a landlocked back lot the developer wants to subdivide, and to collect Maria Q. Fairbanks's unpaid share of the private road maintenance.
fact_pattern.narrative:
the Lakeview Heights plat was platted and the plan recorded in 1949, showing Maple Street as a way running through the development. Maple Street was never built or accepted as a public way; on the ground the lots are served instead by a seasonal private road, which the Lakeview Heights Road Association maintains. Grace C. Roe owns a lot in the subdivision and serves as an officer of the association.

Coastal Outfitters LLC, a developer, owns a landlocked back lot the developer wants to subdivide and now claims a right to open and use Maple Street as access, asserting that the recorded plan grants all lot owners rights in the paper street. Grace contends that under Maine's Paper Streets Law Maple Street was deemed vacated decades ago because the town never accepted it, so the developer has no public way and, the association says, no private easement to push a new road through the neighborhood.

Separately, Maria Q. Fairbanks, a neighbor whose lot is served by a seasonal private road, has refused to pay his proportionate share of the road-maintenance assessments. The association's annual maintenance cost runs about $33699, and Maria's unpaid share has grown to roughly $6524. Grace seeks a declaration that the way is vacated and the developer has no easement, to quiet title, to collect the unpaid assessment with attorney fees, and to enjoin the developer from opening Maple Street.
undisputed_facts: the Lakeview Heights plat was platted and its plan recorded in 1949.
Maple Street was never built and the lots are in fact served by a seasonal private road.
the Lakeview Heights Road Association maintains the actual access road and levies assessments on the lot owners.
the Lakeview Heights Road Association apportions the annual maintenance cost as a share proportionate to each owner's actual use of the way.
disputed_facts: Whether Maple Street is deemed vacated under the Paper Streets Law and, if so, whether any private access rights survive.
Whether Coastal Outfitters LLC holds an easement over the paper street to reach a landlocked back lot the developer wants to subdivide.
Whether the assessment formula is fair and equitable and how much Maria Q. Fairbanks owes for road maintenance.

timeline.event: The plan for the Lakeview Heights plat, showing Maple Street, was recorded.
Complaint to quiet title and for declaratory and injunctive relief prepared for filing in Bath Superior Court.

third_parties.name: Town Road Commissioner
third_parties.relevance: Speaks to whether the town ever accepted the way and to road-standard requirements.

third_parties.name: Desmond D. Poe
third_parties.relevance: Successor to the developer who recorded the original plat and any reserved rights.

third_parties.name: Omar C. Roe
third_parties.role: Other lot owner in the subdivision
third_parties.relevance: Pays road assessments and has an interest in the status of the paper street.

exchanges.question: What are you trying to accomplish?
exchanges.answer: I want it confirmed that Maple Street is a dead paper street and keep the developer from cutting a new road through to a landlocked back lot the developer wants to subdivide. And I want Maria to finally pay his share of the road.

exchanges.question: Tell me about Maple Street.
exchanges.answer: It is only a line on the old 1949 plan. It was never built. We all use a seasonal private road, and the town never took Maple Street over.

exchanges.question: What is the developer claiming?
exchanges.answer: They bought a landlocked back lot the developer wants to subdivide and now say that the recorded plan grants all lot owners rights in the paper street. They want to open that paper street as a road. The whole neighborhood is against it.

exchanges.question: How does the road get maintained and paid for?
exchanges.answer: the Lakeview Heights Road Association runs it. We assess the owners for upkeep -- it is about $33699 a year for grading, gravel, and plowing -- and we split it as a share proportionate to each owner's actual use of the way. Everyone pays but Maria.

exchanges.question: How much is Maria behind?
exchanges.answer: He is into us for about $6524 now. He just refuses, and the rest of us are covering his share of a road he uses every day.

exchanges.question: How do you want to handle this?
exchanges.answer: I would rather settle the road bill than fight forever, but I am not going to let the developer open that street. The survey and the title work back us up.

goals: Confirm that Maple Street is deemed vacated and that Coastal Outfitters LLC has no easement to open it to reach a landlocked back lot the developer wants to subdivide.
Collect Maria Q. Fairbanks's unpaid proportionate share of the road-maintenance assessments, plus attorney fees.
Protect a seasonal private road and the character of the neighborhood from a new through-road.
priorities: Keeping the developer from opening the paper street.
A clean adjudication that the way is vacated and quieting title.
A fair and enforceable road-maintenance cost-sharing arrangement.
desired_outcomes: A declaration and quiet-title judgment that the paper street is vacated and the developer holds no easement over it.
A money judgment for the unpaid road-maintenance share with attorney fees, and an order barring the developer from opening the way.
non_starters: Any outcome that lets the developer build or open a road over Maple Street.

constraints.budget: Moderate; willing to pay for the survey and title work but cost-conscious on the rest.
constraints.timeline: Wants the way's status settled before the developer can start any road or subdivision work.

issues.issue: Private road association maintenance and each owner's fair and equitable proportionate share
sub_questions: Is the assessment a fair and equitable apportionment among benefited owners?
Does the association apportion cost by a defined method (a share proportionate to each owner's actual use of the way)?
May the association recover the unpaid share and attorney fees by civil action?
issues.client_position: The association apportions maintenance as a share proportionate to each owner's actual use of the way, and Maria owes that fair and equitable proportionate share plus attorney fees for non-payment.

issues.issue: Quiet title and declaratory judgment as to the status of the way and the developer's claimed easement
sub_questions: Is a declaration appropriate to settle the status of the way?
Should title be quieted against the developer's claimed easement?

issues.issue: Whether Maple Street is deemed vacated under the Paper Streets Law
sub_questions: Was the way platted before 1987 and never accepted by the municipality?
Was it deemed vacated by the later of September 29, 1997 or 15 years after recording?
issues.client_position: Because the town never accepted Maple Street, it was deemed vacated by operation of the statute.

issues.issue: Whether lot owners retain private access rights over the paper street despite vacation of public rights
sub_questions: Does the doctrine of incipient dedication grant lot owners private rights in the platted way?
Has any such private right been used or abandoned?
issues.client_position: Any incipient private rights do not entitle the developer to open a new road for a back lot.

issues.issue: The rights of action and reservations attending a vacated platted way
sub_questions: What rights survive vacation of the public way?
Did the recorded plan or any deed reserve private access rights?
issues.client_position: No enforceable private easement over the paper street survives in the developer.

expert_opinions.topic: Road standards and feasibility of opening the paper street
expert_opinions.opinion: The paper street corridor can feasibly be built to a road standard adequate to serve the back lot.
expert_opinions.basis: Engineering analysis of grades, drainage, and applicable road standards.

expert_opinions.topic: Chain of title and the recorded plan
expert_opinions.opinion: The chain of title and the recorded plan show no deeded easement reserving the paper street to the developer's back lot.
expert_opinions.basis: Examination of the recorded subdivision plan and the deeds in the chain of title.

evidence.title: Town records on acceptance of the way
evidence.description: The town's records showing acceptance or non-acceptance of the platted way.

evidence.title: Road association bylaws and maintenance assessments
evidence.description: The association's bylaws and the schedule of maintenance assessments.

evidence.title: Chain of deeds for the affected lots
evidence.description: The recorded conveyances for the lots and the claimed easement.

evidence.title: The developer's deed and access claim
evidence.description: The developer's deed to the back lot and its written assertion of access over the paper street.

amounts.label: Value impact of the access dispute on the lots
amounts.basis: Estimated effect of the dispute on the affected lots' market value.

amounts.label: Annual road-maintenance cost
amounts.basis: The association's annual grading, gravel, and plowing budget.

amounts.label: Survey and title-examination costs
amounts.basis: Cost of the boundary survey and the title abstract.

causes_of_action.claim: Declaratory judgment that the paper street is deemed vacated and the developer has no easement
causes_of_action.against: Coastal Outfitters LLC and the Town of Mooselook
elements: A pre-1987 platted way never accepted by the municipality
Vacation by operation of the Paper Streets Law
No surviving private easement in the developer
An actual controversy ripe for declaration
causes_of_action.relief_sought: A declaration that the paper street is deemed vacated and that the developer holds no easement over it

elements: The plaintiff and the association have an interest in the access ways
The developer asserts an adverse claim of easement over the paper street
The adverse claim is without basis in the record title
Title should be quieted against the claim
causes_of_action.relief_sought: A judgment quieting title against the developer's claimed easement over the paper street

causes_of_action.claim: Collection of the unpaid road-maintenance assessment with attorney fees
causes_of_action.by: Grace C. Roe and the Lakeview Heights Road Association
elements: The defendant's lot is served by and benefits from the private way
A fair and equitable proportionate share was assessed
The defendant has failed to pay his share
The association may recover the share and attorney fees by civil action
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Judgment for the unpaid proportionate share of maintenance, plus attorney fees and costs

causes_of_action.claim: Injunction barring the developer from opening the paper street
elements: The paper street is vacated and the developer has no easement
Opening the way would cause irreparable harm to the lot owners
The balance of harms favors the plaintiff
There is no adequate remedy at law
causes_of_action.relief_sought: An order enjoining the developer from constructing or opening a road over the paper street

affirmative_defenses.basis: The plaintiff and the association acquiesced in the platted way and delayed unreasonably in challenging access.
The back lot takes an easement over the paper street by implication from the subdivision plan or by necessity for access.

counterclaims.claim: Easement over the paper street to the back lot
counterclaims.relief: A declaration that the developer holds an easement over the paper street sufficient to access and develop the back lot.

motions.title: Motion for summary judgment on the vacation of the way
motions.relief: Summary judgment that the paper street is deemed vacated and the developer has no easement.

motions.title: Motion to appoint a referee on the assessment
motions.relief: Appointment of a referee to determine the fair and equitable share owed.

motions.title: Motion for a preliminary injunction
motions.relief: A preliminary injunction barring road construction over the paper street pending judgment.

docket.entry: Complaint to quiet title and for declaratory and injunctive relief filed.
Answer and counterclaim asserting an easement filed by the developer.
Answer of the defendant neighbor on the assessment filed.
Order on the motion for a preliminary injunction entered.
Scheduling order entered and the Town appears on the way's status.
Hearing held on summary judgment as to vacation of the way.

facts.cost_sharing_basis: a share proportionate to each owner's actual use of the way
facts.developer_back_lot: a landlocked back lot the developer wants to subdivide
facts.developer_access_theory: that the recorded plan grants all lot owners rights in the paper street
